title: The pitfalls of Hungary's post-Orban transition
subhead: Riding a powerful wave of opposition to the outgoing government, Hungary's new prime minister, Péter Magyar, now has to balance the expectations of both left- and right-wing voters while clarifying his positions, especially regarding Ukraine.

What is the controversial bill on antisemitism French MPs are debating?
subhead: MP Caroline Yadan has been accused of trying to silence criticism of Israel with her bill, which aims to 'combat new forms of antisemitism.' A petition against the text has gathered over 700,000 signatures.

title: 'Why not try to remobilize a France that has faith in its future?'
subhead: To counter a collective sense of powerlessness, the worst poison for any democracy, political scientist François Godard proposes to build a platform restoring the State's capacity by rallying engineers and other driving forces across the country.

title: At Lyon's Musée des Confluences, the Loeb donation celebrates Mali's living arts
subhead: More than 100 masks, puppets and instruments are displayed in a way that evokes the singing, dancing and movements that once accompanied ceremonies along the Niger River.

title: Rapper Gims linked to international money laundering ring
subhead: Investigators uncovered nearly €8 million in suspicious transactions involving Gims, his inner circle, and an international criminal organization. The Congolese music star has been charged. He denies the accusations

title: Jordan Bardella, a princess and 'Paris Match': The story of a PR gamble
subhead: The relationship between Marine Le Pen's lieutenant and an aristocratic student raised in the international jet set made the front page of 'Paris Match.' The media empire of Vincent Bolloré has seized the opportunity to 'glamorize' the likely far-right presidential candidate.

title: Why the Hormuz 'mission' led by France and the UK is largely symbolic
subhead: Wanting to avoid being drawn into the US's blockade of the key shipping route, Macron and Starmer aim to establish a multilateral and 'strictly defensive' plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz.
